description: Create a content slide on AI adoption in financial services with a left sidebar text card and a right-side two-column horizontal bar chart showing the top AI/GenAI and ML use cases in risk management.

header.kicker: SECOND ANNUAL EY-IIF GLOBAL INSURANCE RISK MANAGEMENT SURVEY
header.headline: The state of AI adoption in financial services
header.sub: AI deployments in risk management so far have focused on threat monitoring, surveillance and other internally facing and operational applications. AI usage for core risk management functions – risk identification, assessment, control testing, reporting – are relatively rare, with between 10% and 20% of CRO insurance survey respondents saying they are using AI for those tasks. Relatively few CROs say they are using or considering AI for these risk activities today, but there’s every reason to believe that adoption will increase in the future, once first-generation deployments are complete and the potential advantages become clearer to more CROs.

sidebar.title: The state of AI adoption in financial services
sidebar.body:
The annual EY-IIF research into AI usage in financial services charts the pace and trajectory of AI and ML by financial services firms, including insurers. Not only are all firms increasing their investments, according to our research, but the vast majority (88%) of firms already have applications in production. The most common deployments of predictive AI applications are in risk, fraud, operations and compliance functions. CROs and chief data officers (CDOs) are usually responsible for overseeing governance.

Download the full report.
